normalized_title: City of Fremantle Local Planning Policy 2.23 - Register of Significant Trees and Vegetation Areas
scope_summary: Defines criteria for including or removing trees and vegetation areas on the City of Fremantle's Significant Trees and Vegetation Areas Register, and guides assessment of development applications affecting registered trees. Applies to land zoned under Local Planning Scheme No. 4; nominations require landowner consent and are assessed against viability and significance criteria (botanic, visual/landmark, heritage, ecological). Removal through development is only permissible for infrastructure damage/safety, poor health, or where retention would preclude permissible development, with AS 4970-2009 tree protection compliance required on development sites.

key_numeric_standards.topic: minimum height for juvenile tree nomination
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: juvenile trees nominated for potential future significance (must also be endemic and listed in the Urban Forest Plan)

cross_references.cited: Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015 (Schedule 2 Deemed Provisions)
Local Planning Scheme No. 4 (LPS4), Schedule A clause 13A
Local Planning Policy 1.7 - Development Exempt from Approval Under Local Planning Scheme No. 4
AS 4970-2009 Protection of trees on development sites
